why_it_matters: Family history of heart disease, diabetes, and cancer is a core element of a first comprehensive exam and informs risk stratification—especially in a patient with obesity—so its unknown status should be explicitly recorded.
Uninsured status materially affects feasibility of referrals, follow-up, and testing, making it important to document for care planning.
The PHQ-2 score substantiates the documented depression screening and its below-threshold result, supporting completeness though not changing management.
Routine post-vaccination soreness advice is standard aftercare with negligible clinical impact if omitted.
